NKJV.1: Oh come, let us sing to the LORD! Let us shout joyfully to the Rock of our salvation.
NKJV.2: Let us come before His presence with thanksgiving; Let us shout joyfully to Him with psalms.
NKJV.3: For the LORD is the great God, And the great King above all gods.
NKJV.4: In His hand are the deep places of the earth; The heights of the hills are His also.
NKJV.5: The sea is His, for He made it; And His hands formed the dry land.
NKJV.6: Oh come, let us worship and bow down; Let us kneel before the LORD our Maker.
NKJV.7: For He is our God, And we are the people of His pasture, And the sheep of His hand. Today, if you will hear His voice:
NKJV.8: “Do not harden your hearts, as in the rebellion, As in the day of trial in the wilderness,
NKJV.9: When your fathers tested Me; They tried Me, though they saw My work.
NKJV.10: For forty years I was grieved with that generation, And said, ‘It is a people who go astray in their hearts, And they do not know My ways.’
NKJV.11: So I swore in My wrath, ‘They shall not enter My rest.’ ”

ESV.1: Oh come, let us sing to the LORD; let us make a joyful noise to the rock of our salvation!
ESV.2: Let us come into his presence with thanksgiving; let us make a joyful noise to him with songs of praise!
ESV.3: For the LORD is a great God, and a great King above all gods.
ESV.4: In his hand are the depths of the earth; the heights of the mountains are his also.
ESV.5: The sea is his, for he made it, and his hands formed the dry land.
ESV.6: Oh come, let us worship and bow down; let us kneel before the LORD, our Maker!
ESV.7: For he is our God, and we are the people of his pasture, and the sheep of his hand. Today, if you hear his voice,
ESV.8: do not harden your hearts, as at Meribah, as on the day at Massah in the wilderness,
ESV.9: when your fathers put me to the test and put me to the proof, though they had seen my work.
ESV.10: For forty years I loathed that generation and said, “They are a people who go astray in their heart, and they have not known my ways.”
ESV.11: Therefore I swore in my wrath, “They shall not enter my rest.”

NASB.1: O come, let us sing for joy to the LORD, Let us shout joyfully to the rock of our salvation.
NASB.2: Let us come before His presence with thanksgiving, Let us shout joyfully to Him with psalms.
NASB.3: For the LORD is a great God And a great King above all gods,
NASB.4: In whose hand are the depths of the earth, The peaks of the mountains are His also.
NASB.5: The sea is His, for it was He who made it, And His hands formed the dry land.
NASB.6: Come, let us worship and bow down, Let us kneel before the LORD our Maker.
NASB.7: For He is our God, And we are the people of His pasture and the sheep of His hand. Today, if you would hear His voice,
NASB.8: Do not harden your hearts, as at Meribah, As in the day of Massah in the wilderness,
NASB.9: 'When your fathers tested Me, They tried Me, though they had seen My work.
NASB.10: 'For forty years I loathed [that] generation, And said they are a people who err in their heart, And they do not know My ways.
NASB.11: 'Therefore I swore in My anger, Truly they shall not enter into My rest.'

NLT.1:
Come, let us sing to the LORD!
Let us shout joyfully to the Rock of our salvation.
NLT.2:
Let us come to him with thanksgiving.
Let us sing psalms of praise to him.
NLT.3:
For the LORD is a great God,
a great King above all gods.
NLT.4:
He holds in his hands the depths of the earth
and the mightiest mountains.
NLT.5:
The sea belongs to him, for he made it.
His hands formed the dry land, too.
NLT.6:
Come, let us worship and bow down.
Let us kneel before the LORD our maker,
NLT.7:
for he is our God.
We are the people he watches over,
the flock under his care.
If only you would listen to his voice today!
NLT.8:
The LORD says, “Don’t harden your hearts as Israel did at Meribah,
as they did at Massah in the wilderness.
NLT.9:
For there your ancestors tested and tried my patience,
even though they saw everything I did.
NLT.10:
For forty years I was angry with them, and I said,
‘They are a people whose hearts turn away from me.
They refuse to do what I tell them.’
NLT.11:
So in my anger I took an oath:
‘They will never enter my place of rest.’”

WEB.1: Oh come, let’s sing to Yahweh. Let’s shout aloud to the rock of our salvation!
WEB.2: Let’s come before his presence with thanksgiving. Let’s extol him with songs!
WEB.3: For Yahweh is a great God, a great King above all gods.
WEB.4: In his hand are the deep places of the earth. The heights of the mountains are also his.
WEB.5: The sea is his, and he made it. His hands formed the dry land.
WEB.6: Oh come, let’s worship and bow down. Let’s kneel before Yahweh, our Maker,
WEB.7: for he is our God. We are the people of his pasture, and the sheep in his care. Today, oh that you would hear his voice!
WEB.8: Don’t harden your heart, as at Meribah, as in the day of Massah in the wilderness,
WEB.9: when your fathers tempted me, tested me, and saw my work.
WEB.10: Forty long years I was grieved with that generation, and said, “It is a people that errs in their heart. They have not known my ways.”
WEB.11: Therefore I swore in my wrath, “They won’t enter into my rest.”

AMP.1:
O come, let us sing joyfully to the LORD;
Let us shout joyfully to the rock of our salvation.
AMP.2:
Let us come before His presence with a song of thanksgiving;
Let us shout joyfully to Him with songs.
AMP.3:
For the LORD is a great God
And a great King above all gods,
AMP.4:
In whose hand are the depths of the earth;
The peaks of the mountains are His also.
AMP.5:
The sea is His, for He made it [by His command];
And His hands formed the dry land.
AMP.6:
O come, let us worship and bow down,
Let us kneel before the LORD our Maker [in reverent praise and prayer].
AMP.7:
For He is our God
And we are the people of His pasture and the sheep of His hand.
Today, if you will hear His voice,
AMP.8:
Do not harden your hearts and become spiritually dull as at Meribah [the place of strife],
And as at Massah [the place of testing] in the wilderness,
AMP.9:
"When your fathers tested Me,
They tried Me, even though they had seen My work [of miracles].
AMP.10:
"For forty years I was grieved and disgusted with that generation,
And I said, 'They are a people who err in their heart,
And they do not acknowledge or regard My ways.'
AMP.11:
"Therefore I swore [an oath] in My wrath,
'They absolutely shall not enter My rest [the land of promise].'"

MSG.1:
Come, let's shout praises to God,
raise the roof for the Rock who saved us!
MSG.2:
Let's march into his presence singing praises,
lifting the rafters with our hymns!
MSG.3:
And why? Because God is the best,
High King over all the gods.
MSG.4:
In one hand he holds deep caves and caverns,
in the other hand grasps the high mountains.
MSG.5:
He made Ocean—he owns it!
His hands sculpted Earth!
MSG.6:
So come, let us worship: bow before him,
on your knees before God, who made us!
MSG.7:
Oh yes, he's our God,
and we're the people he pastures, the flock he feeds.
Drop everything and listen, listen as he speaks:
MSG.8:
"Don't turn a deaf ear as in the Bitter Uprising,
As on the day of the Wilderness Test,
MSG.9: when your ancestors turned and put me to the test.
MSG.10:
For forty years they watched me at work among them,
as over and over they tried my patience.
And I was provoked—oh, was I provoked!
'Can't they keep their minds on God for five minutes?
Do they simply refuse to walk down my road?'
MSG.11:
Exasperated, I exploded,
'They'll never get where they're headed,
never be able to sit down and rest.' "

NIV.1:
Psalm 95
Come, let us sing for joy to the Lord;
let us shout aloud to the Rock of our salvation.
NIV.2:
Let us come before him with thanksgiving
and extol him with music and song.
NIV.3:
For the Lord is the great God,
the great King above all gods.
NIV.4:
In his hand are the depths of the earth,
and the mountain peaks belong to him.
NIV.5:
The sea is his, for he made it,
and his hands formed the dry land.
NIV.6:
Come, let us bow down in worship,
let us kneel before the Lord our Maker;
NIV.7:
for he is our God
and we are the people of his pasture,
the flock under his care.
Today, if you hear his voice,
NIV.8:
do not harden your hearts as you did at Meribah,
as you did that day at Massah in the desert,
NIV.9:
where your fathers tested and tried me,
though they had seen what I did.
NIV.10:
For forty years I was angry with that generation;
I said, “They are a people whose hearts go astray,
and they have not known my ways.”
NIV.11:
So I declared on oath in my anger,
“They shall never enter my rest.”

NRSVCE.1:
O come, let us sing to the LORD;
let us make a joyful noise to the rock of our salvation!
NRSVCE.2:
Let us come into his presence with thanksgiving;
let us make a joyful noise to him with songs of praise!
NRSVCE.3:
For the LORD is a great God,
and a great King above all gods.
NRSVCE.4:
In his hand are the depths of the earth;
the heights of the mountains are his also.
NRSVCE.5:
The sea is his, for he made it,
and the dry land, which his hands have formed.
NRSVCE.6:
O come, let us worship and bow down,
let us kneel before the LORD, our Maker!
NRSVCE.7:
For he is our God,
and we are the people of his pasture,
and the sheep of his hand.
O that today you would listen to his voice!
NRSVCE.8:
Do not harden your hearts, as at Meribah,
as on the day at Massah in the wilderness,
NRSVCE.9:
when your ancestors tested me,
and put me to the proof, though they had seen my work.
NRSVCE.10:
For forty years I loathed that generation
and said, “They are a people whose hearts go astray,
and they do not regard my ways.”
NRSVCE.11:
Therefore in my anger I swore,
“They shall not enter my rest.”

NET.1: Come! Let’s sing for joy to the LORD! Let’s shout out praises to our protector who delivers us!
NET.2: Let’s enter his presence with thanksgiving! Let’s shout out to him in celebration!
NET.3: For the LORD is a great God, a great king who is superior to all gods.
NET.4: The depths of the earth are in his hand, and the mountain peaks belong to him.
NET.5: The sea is his, for he made it. His hands formed the dry land.
NET.6: Come! Let’s bow down and worship! Let’s kneel before the LORD, our creator!
NET.7: For he is our God; we are the people of his pasture, the sheep he owns. Today, if only you would obey him!
NET.8: He says, “Do not be stubborn like they were at Meribah, like they were that day at Massah in the wilderness,
NET.9: where your ancestors challenged my authority, and tried my patience, even though they had seen my work.
NET.10: For forty years I was continually disgusted with that generation, and I said, ‘These people desire to go astray; they do not obey my commands.’
NET.11: So I made a vow in my anger, ‘They will never enter into the resting place I had set aside for them.’”
